prompt: Help me understand what each function in extractor/text_utils.py does by creating runnable example files for every function

assertions.text: Every function in text_utils.py has a corresponding .py file (9 functions: strip_images, strip_urls, strip_references, strip_acknowledgments, strip_extra_blanks, _split_sections, _classify_headings_with_llm, extract_relevant_sections, preprocess_md)
Every generated .py file runs successfully with `python <file>` (exit code 0)
_classify_headings_with_llm.py uses a mock instead of making real API calls, yet still demonstrates the function's behavior
Sample inputs use realistic scientific paper markdown content with real-looking headings, image tags, URLs, etc.
Each file has a docstring explaining the function's purpose
